scenario_results.evidence: Manifest scope, supported tasks, and official ServiceNow, Atlassian, Freshservice, and BMC sources support bounded incident-routing and queue-configuration guidance.
The package includes SLA implementation review in scope while explicitly rejecting enterprise KPI ownership outside the platform lane.
Native workflow and approval review is in scope, and custom scripting ownership is rejected in manifest boundary controls.
Official Integration Hub, Atlassian Assets, Freshservice API, and BMC integration sources support platform-side integration-constraint guidance and escalation boundaries.
Change-module implementation guidance is explicitly in scope while broader change-governance ownership is rejected.
Lifecycle and release-readiness handling is documented in the manifest, deployment package, and readiness evidence with rollback and monitoring expectations.
Role, workspace, and access-model governance is bounded to the ITSM platform layer with identity and organizational design escalation retained.
Edge-case plugin and workflow collisions are covered with reversible remediation and unknown-customization escalation.
Manifest and readiness artifacts require explicit uncertainty, missing-context requests, and conservative escalation instead of guessed fixes.
Custom software engineering and bespoke middleware requests are clearly rejected and routed to software or integration specialists.
Manifest adjacency map and routing rules constrain the lane and reject service-catalog ownership, CMDB governance, and service-desk staffing.
Shared-meta-agent contracts and fallback rules allow safe startup and release review with deferred delegated normalization.

notes: Results are evidence-based and derived from the manifest, package artifacts, and repository delivery contract for issue #138.
Acceptance thresholds are exceeded with 12 scenarios, 12 passes, and a 1.00 pass rate against a 0.92 threshold.
Low-confidence and adjacent-specialty cases are routed rather than guessed.
